stack: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js v2 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4
summary: Production-grade SEO implementation with comprehensive structured data, dynamic sitemap, robots.txt, llms.txt, per-page meta tags on 39 pages, and SSR-ready architecture. Key gaps: missing OG images (referenced but not on disk), no default meta description in base template, missing canonical URLs on 6 high-value public pages, no responsive image attributes (srcset/sizes), no Brotli compression, and no pagination SEO signals.

findings.title: No default meta description in base Blade template
findings.description: app.blade.php has no <meta name="description"> fallback. If a page component fails to set one via Inertia Head, search engines get no description. Google may auto-generate snippets but they'll be suboptimal.
findings.recommendation: Add a default meta description in the <head> that per-page Inertia Head components can override.

findings.title: No default og:description in base template
findings.description: Open Graph tags in app.blade.php include og:type, og:site_name, og:url, og:image but not og:description. Social shares without per-page overrides will show no description preview.
findings.recommendation: Add a default og:description meta tag alongside the existing OG tags.

findings.title: Missing canonical URL overrides on 6 public pages
findings.description: Welcome, About, Contact, Pricing, Changelog, and Roadmap pages don't set a per-page canonical via Inertia Head. They rely on the base template's request()->url() canonical which works but won't normalize query strings or trailing slashes.
findings.recommendation: The base template canonical (line 8) provides adequate coverage. For robustness, pass canonicalUrl from controllers for these pages to match the pattern used on all other public pages.

findings.title: Duplicate canonical tag potential on pages with Inertia Head override
findings.description: The base Blade template always emits <link rel="canonical"> (line 8). Pages that also emit a canonical via Inertia Head may produce two canonical tags. Inertia's Head component deduplicates by head-key, but only if the component uses the same element type.
findings.recommendation: Verify that Inertia's deduplication handles the Blade-emitted canonical correctly. If not, conditionally emit the base canonical only when no page-level override exists.

findings.title: Auth/Register pages have meta descriptions
findings.description: Login and Register pages include meta descriptions which is good for SEO if these pages are intended to be indexed. However, robots.txt disallows /login and /register, creating a mixed signal.
findings.recommendation: Decide whether auth pages should be indexed. If not (current robots.txt stance), the meta descriptions are harmless but unnecessary.

strengths: Dynamic title tags via Inertia Head on all pages
39 pages have per-page meta descriptions
Conditional robots meta: noindex for authenticated users, full index for public
Dual-layer noindex: meta tag + X-Robots-Tag HTTP header for authenticated pages
OG and Twitter Card tags with summary_large_image on all public pages
google-extended meta tag for AI crawler control
html lang attribute set dynamically from app locale

findings.title: BlogPosting schema missing author details
findings.description: Blog posts use BlogPosting schema with author as Organization only. Google recommends Person type for author with name, url, and image properties for better rich result eligibility.
findings.recommendation: Add author as Person type with name and url if individual author attribution is relevant.

findings.title: SoftwareApplication schema has hardcoded screenshot URL
findings.description: The SoftwareApplication JSON-LD references a screenshot URL that should be verified to exist and be accessible.
findings.recommendation: Verify the screenshot URL resolves and consider adding multiple screenshots for richer results.

findings.title: No Product schema on pricing/buy pages
findings.description: The pricing and buy pages could benefit from Product or Offer schema markup for rich snippets showing price in search results.
findings.recommendation: Add Product or SoftwareApplication+Offer schema to pricing/buy pages.

strengths: 5 schema types implemented: SoftwareApplication, Organization, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, BlogPosting
Article schema on long-form guide pages
BreadcrumbJsonLd component used on 28 pages with safe script tag escaping
FaqJsonLd component on feature pages with dynamic Q&A data
Server-side JSON-LD in Blade for homepage (SoftwareApplication, Organization, FAQPage)
Dynamic pricing in SoftwareApplication offers when billing enabled
Proper @context and @type declarations on all schemas

findings.title: Sitemap lastmod dates are hardcoded for static pages
findings.description: 35+ sitemap URLs use hardcoded lastmod timestamps (e.g., '2026-03-01T00:00:00+00:00'). Only blog posts use actual file modification times. Stale lastmod dates reduce crawl efficiency signals.
findings.recommendation: Consider using deployment timestamps or git commit dates for lastmod on static pages. Blog posts already use file mtime (good).

findings.title: No trailing slash normalization middleware
findings.description: No middleware enforces consistent trailing slash behavior. URLs with and without trailing slashes may both resolve, creating potential duplicate content signals (mitigated by base canonical tag).
findings.recommendation: Add middleware to 301-redirect trailing-slash URLs to non-trailing-slash versions, or vice versa.

findings.title: No sitemap index for future scalability
findings.description: Currently ~40 URLs in a single sitemap.xml. As blog/guide content grows, may exceed single-file limits (50,000 URLs).
findings.recommendation: Not urgent. Consider sitemap index when approaching 1000+ URLs.

findings.title: Missing /buy page from sitemap.xml
findings.description: The /buy page is a public route with meta tags but is not included in the sitemap.
findings.recommendation: Add /buy to the sitemap if it should be indexed.

strengths: Dynamic robots.txt with environment awareness (blocks all in non-production)
Comprehensive disallow rules for authenticated/admin routes
24-hour cached XML sitemap with 40+ URLs
Feature-flag-aware sitemap (pricing included only when billing enabled)
Blog posts use real file mtime for lastmod
llms.txt implementation following llmstxt.org spec
Clean semantic URLs on all content pages (no query params)
Proper 404 error page with navigation links
SSR-ready architecture ensures content available without JavaScript
Sitemap reference in robots.txt

findings.title: No responsive image attributes (srcset/sizes)
findings.description: No images in the codebase use srcset or sizes attributes. Mobile devices download full-size images unnecessarily, impacting LCP and bandwidth. Marketing screenshots and OG images are affected.
findings.recommendation: Add srcset and sizes attributes to the FeatureScreenshot component and any hero/above-fold images. Consider generating multiple image sizes at build time.

findings.title: No Brotli compression configured
findings.description: Nginx config only enables gzip. Brotli provides 15-20% better compression ratios for text assets, improving TTFB and overall page weight.
findings.recommendation: Add Brotli compression alongside gzip in nginx configuration.

findings.title: No DNS prefetch for external services
findings.description: Only fonts.bunny.net has a preconnect hint. Google Analytics, Stripe JS, and other external services loaded at runtime have no DNS prefetch, adding latency on first use.
findings.recommendation: Add dns-prefetch for googletagmanager.com, google-analytics.com, js.stripe.com, and m.stripe.network.

findings.title: No critical CSS inlining for above-fold content
findings.description: CSS is loaded as a blocking external stylesheet. Inlining critical above-fold CSS would improve FCP by eliminating a render-blocking request.
findings.recommendation: Extract and inline critical CSS for landing pages. Low priority since Tailwind output is typically small (~8-12KB gzipped).

findings.title: No WebP/AVIF image format usage
findings.description: Marketing images are SVG (good for vectors) but OG images and screenshots reference PNG format only. WebP provides 25-35% size reduction over PNG.
findings.recommendation: Generate WebP versions of raster images. SVG usage for feature illustrations is already optimal.

findings.title: Font preload for critical weight missing
findings.description: Inter 400 weight is used on all pages but only preconnect is specified, not preload. Explicit preload of the primary weight would reduce font swap flash.
findings.recommendation: Add <link rel="preload" as="font"> for Inter 400 woff2.

strengths: Sophisticated Vite vendor chunking (7 chunk groups) preventing monolithic bundles
Lazy-loaded global components (Toaster, CookieConsent, FlashToasts, Tooltip) with Suspense
Inertia SSR support with dual-mode hydration (hydrateRoot/createRoot fallback)
Google Analytics loaded asynchronously with cookie consent gating
Font-display: swap prevents invisible text during font load
1-year immutable cache on versioned build assets via nginx
Image lazy loading with loading="lazy" and decoding="async"
Width/height attributes on FeatureScreenshot to prevent CLS
Tailwind v4 auto-purging produces minimal CSS output
Production builds drop console/debugger statements
Reduced motion support (prefers-reduced-motion)
Gzip compression at level 6 with proper Vary header

findings.title: Blog has only 3 posts
findings.description: The markdown-based blog system is well-implemented but has minimal content. Consistent publishing would significantly improve organic search presence for long-tail keywords.
findings.recommendation: Establish a content calendar. The existing posts target high-value keywords (feature flags, Redis locks, admin panels). Continue this pattern.

findings.title: Markdown renderer lacks advanced formatting
findings.description: BlogController's markdown-to-HTML renderer handles headings, bold, italic, code, links, and paragraphs but lacks tables, blockquotes, ordered lists, and fenced code blocks with syntax highlighting.
findings.recommendation: Consider using a full Markdown parser (league/commonmark) for richer blog content rendering.

findings.title: No content freshness signals on guide pages
findings.description: Guide pages don't display last-updated dates or version numbers. Adding these signals helps search engines assess content freshness.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Last updated' dates to guide pages, especially the comparison and tutorial guides.

strengths: 39 unique page titles with strategic keyword targeting
39 unique meta descriptions across all public pages
Competitor keyword targeting in compare pages (Jetstream, Spark, SaaSykit, Wave, etc.)
Long-tail keywords in guide titles (e.g., 'Laravel Feature Flags Without LaunchDarkly')
Proper H1-H2-H3 heading hierarchy on all pages (single H1 per page)
Comprehensive alt text on all images (descriptive, not keyword-stuffed)
9 comparison pages targeting competitor search queries
9 long-form guides (18-25 min reading time) targeting tutorial keywords
FAQ sections on feature pages (eligible for FAQ rich results)
Internal linking via RelatedContent component on blog and feature pages
Descriptive anchor text throughout (no generic 'click here' links)
Content hubs (features, compare, guides, blog) with hub-spoke architecture

findings.title: Referenced OG images do not exist on disk
findings.description: Controllers pass ogImage paths like 'og/features-billing.png', 'og/guide-saas.png', and 'og-image.png' but these files don't exist in the public directory. Only public/images/og-default.png exists. Social shares from feature/guide/blog pages will show broken or fallback images.
findings.recommendation: Create OG images for all referenced paths (1200x630px recommended). Use a template system for consistency. Alternatively, implement dynamic OG image generation.

findings.title: Compare pages have no OG images
findings.description: All 9 comparison pages pass no ogImage property. Social shares will fall back to the default OG image which isn't comparison-specific.
findings.recommendation: Add OG images for comparison pages, ideally showing the comparison matchup visually.

findings.title: No twitter:site or twitter:creator meta tags
findings.description: Twitter Card tags include twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, and twitter:image but not twitter:site or twitter:creator. These associate shares with the project's Twitter account.
findings.recommendation: Add twitter:site meta tag with the project's Twitter handle when one exists.

findings.title: No social share buttons on content pages
findings.description: Blog posts, guides, and comparison pages have no share buttons for Twitter, LinkedIn, or other platforms. Adding share functionality could increase organic backlinks.
findings.recommendation: Add lightweight share buttons (using URL-based sharing, not heavy SDKs) to blog and guide pages.

strengths: Open Graph tags (type, site_name, url, image) on all pages via base template
Twitter Card (summary_large_image) configured globally
Per-page OG title, description, and type overrides via Inertia Head
Blog posts use og:type 'article' for proper social card rendering
Default fallback OG image exists (public/images/og-default.png)

findings.title: No www vs non-www redirect enforcement
findings.description: No server-side redirect enforces canonical domain (www vs non-www). Both may resolve if DNS allows, creating duplicate content signals despite the canonical tag.
findings.recommendation: Add nginx redirect rule or Laravel middleware to 301-redirect the non-preferred domain variant.

findings.title: No rel=prev/next for pagination
findings.description: Admin and potentially public paginated views have no rel=prev/next links. While Google deprecated these as ranking signals, they still help crawlers discover paginated content.
findings.recommendation: Add rel=prev/next to any user-facing paginated content. Admin-only pagination doesn't need this.

findings.title: No ETag or Last-Modified headers on HTML responses
findings.description: HTML responses rely on default Laravel cache headers. Adding ETag or Last-Modified enables conditional requests (304 Not Modified), reducing bandwidth and improving crawl efficiency.
findings.recommendation: Add ETag middleware for public pages. Low priority as content changes infrequently.

findings.title: No hreflang tags
findings.description: No multi-language support configured. Single-language (English) site. Not a current issue but blocks international SEO if expansion is planned.
findings.recommendation: Implement when multi-language support is needed. Not required for English-only sites.

findings.title: HSTS preload not enabled by default
findings.description: HSTS is configured with max-age=31536000 and includeSubDomains but preload is gated behind HSTS_PRELOAD env var. Preloading ensures browsers never make an insecure request.
findings.recommendation: Enable HSTS preload after verifying all subdomains support HTTPS, then submit to hstspreload.org.

findings.title: Cookie consent implementation is SEO-compatible
findings.description: CookieConsent component gates Google Analytics on user consent. This is GDPR-compliant but means analytics data may be incomplete for EU visitors.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. This is correct behavior for privacy compliance.

strengths: HTTPS enforced in production via URL::forceScheme('https')
HSTS with 1-year max-age and includeSubDomains
Comprehensive security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
CSP with nonce-based script security
1-year immutable cache on versioned build assets
SSR-ready with Inertia.js (server-rendered HTML for crawlers)
Proper HTTP status codes (401, 403, 404, 429, 500, 503)
UTM parameter capture middleware for attribution tracking
Dynamic locale support (html lang attribute)
Asset versioning via Vite content hashing

top_priorities.title: Create missing OG images
top_priorities.impact: HIGH — Social shares from 15+ pages show broken/fallback images
top_priorities.effort: MEDIUM — Need to create ~10 OG images (1200x630px)
top_priorities.recommendation: Generate OG images for all feature, guide, and blog pages. Consider a template-based approach or dynamic OG image generation service.

top_priorities.title: Add default meta description to base template
top_priorities.impact: HIGH — Pages without per-page description have no fallback
top_priorities.effort: LOW — Single line addition to app.blade.php
top_priorities.recommendation: Add <meta name="description" content="..."> with a generic site description to the base Blade template.

top_priorities.title: Add default og:description to base template
top_priorities.impact: HIGH — Social shares without per-page override show no description
top_priorities.effort: LOW — Single line addition to app.blade.php
top_priorities.recommendation: Add <meta property="og:description" content="..."> alongside existing OG tags.

top_priorities.title: Add responsive image attributes
top_priorities.impact: HIGH — Mobile performance affected by full-size image downloads
top_priorities.effort: MEDIUM — Update FeatureScreenshot component, generate image variants
top_priorities.recommendation: Add srcset and sizes to FeatureScreenshot and hero images.

top_priorities.title: Enable Brotli compression
top_priorities.impact: MEDIUM — 15-20% size reduction over gzip for all text assets
top_priorities.effort: LOW — Add Brotli config to nginx
top_priorities.recommendation: Add brotli_static on and brotli compression config alongside existing gzip.

page_coverage.note: All 42 public pages have base-level canonical via Blade template (request()->url()). 23 pages additionally set explicit canonicalUrl via Inertia props.
